title: Pickled Crunchy Slovakian Grape Leaves Wrap
description: A tangy and crunchy Slovakian dish featuring grape leaves, pickled in a kosher-friendly nuka-pickle and wrapped in a corn husk.

ingredients.name: Grape leaves
ingredients.notes: fresh or frozen, preferably from the Slovakian region

instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and blanch the grape leaves for 30 seconds to remove bitterness. Drain and set aside.
Cut the corn husks in half lengthwise and brush with vegetable oil to prevent sticking.
In a separate pot, make the nuka-pickle brine by combining 1 cup of water, 1 cup of white vinegar, and 1 tsp of kosher salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
Pack the grape leaves into the corn husk halves, overlapping them slightly to create a tight fit. Brush with the nuka-pickle brine and sprinkle with salt.
Roll the grape leaf and corn husk bundle into a tight cylinder and secure with a toothpick. Repeat with remaining grape leaves and corn husks.
Place the bundles seam-side down on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
To serve, remove the toothpicks and garnish with chopped fresh dill. Serve chilled or at room temperature.

Note: Nuka-pickle brine recipe: In a large jar, combine 1 cup of water, 1 cup of white vinegar, and 1 tsp of kosher salt. Add 1 tsp of sugar and 1 tsp of black peppercorns. Stir to combine and refrigerate for at least 24 hours before using.
